title: carlina hispanica subsp. hispanica
intro: Carlina hispanica subsp. hispanica, also known as the Spanish thistle or false sowthistle, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. This perennial herb is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Carlina hispanica subsp. hispanica is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and other conditions.
